title: leontodon scopulorum
intro: Leontodon scopulorum, also known as Mountain Hawk's Beard or Scouler's Hawk's Beard, is a perennial plant species in the dandelion family (Asteraceae). It has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, particularly its roots, which contain inulin and sesquiterpenes, exhibiting anti-inflammatory and antioxidant activities. The plant is believed to have several health benefits, including reducing inflammation, improving digestion, and supporting immune function. Traditionally, the roots of Leontodon scopulorum were used in herbal preparations, such as teas, infusions, and tinctures, to take advantage of its medicinal properties.
